About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.
Organizing body
Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(
lead organizer): MUC
is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
(CAIS-26)
. MUC is a respected higher education institution committed to advancing scientific research and fostering cooperation between academia and industry in fields including cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. This role reflects the college’s mission to support innovation, knowledge exchange, and capacity building within Iraq and the broader scientific community.
Systems (Zesys)
,
(a partner):
the Center for the Advancement of Embedded Systems (often associated with Zesys) is an organization focused on embedded systems research, education, and technology innovation. It works to advance hardware-software integration, support smart system development, and foster industry-academia collaboration. Its partnership with CAIS-26 strengthens the conference’s technical scope, particularly in areas where cybersecurity intersects with intelligent and embedded computing technologies.
Iraqi Association for Libraries and Information
(Partner)
:
the Iraqi Association for Libraries and Information is a professional society dedicated to supporting library and information science in Iraq. The group brings together specialists in information management, documentation, and librarianship to promote best practices, professional development, and collaboration across educational and research institutions. Their involvement in CAIS-26 underscores the importance of information access and management in academic and technical research contexts.
National Islamic Bank
(Sponsor)
:
National Islamic Bank (NIB) is a private Islamic financial institution in Iraq licensed by the Iraqi Central Bank, providing a wide range of Sharia-compliant banking services to individuals and businesses. Established in 2005, it focuses on contributing to economic development through innovative financial solutions, transparent services, and ethical banking practices. NIB’s support for CAIS-26 highlights its interest in community development and investment in knowledge-based initiatives.

Program
Program
However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

Airport pick‑up and drop‑off.
Ground transportation for all listed places in the conference program.
Daily meals for international authors during stay (breakfast, lunch).
On‑site coordination and local support.
Not Included

Place and Accommodation
Conference Venue
The
3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
will be hosted at the
Al Mansour Melia Hotel
, one of Baghdad’s most prestigious and distinguished hotels. Situated in the heart of the Iraqi capital, directly on the banks of the
Tigris River
, the hotel offers an exceptional setting that reflects both the cultural depth and the modern development of the city.
Renowned for its
elegant architectural design
, Al Mansour Melia Hotel combines luxury, comfort, and functionality, making it an ideal venue for an international scientific conference. Its refined interiors, professional conference facilities, and panoramic river views create an inspiring environment that supports academic dialogue, networking, and collaboration among researchers and professionals. The hotel’s tranquil riverfront atmosphere provides a perfect balance between productivity and relaxation, ensuring that participants enjoy a comfortable and engaging conference experience throughout their stay.
Al Mansour Melia Hotel is located in a
vibrant and central area of Baghdad
, offering convenient access to many of the city’s key cultural, governmental, and commercial landmarks. Its strategic location allows conference participants to easily reach the venue from different parts of the city, whether arriving from the airport or local accommodations.
The surrounding area features essential services, dining options, and nearby attractions, making it a practical and enjoyable choice for both local and international attendees. The hotel’s location also provides smooth transportation connections, ensuring ease of movement before, during, and after the conference sessions. This distinguished venue reflects the international status of
CAIS
and reinforces the conference’s commitment to providing a professional, comfortable, and memorable experience for all participants.

Registration and Submission
The 3
rd
international conference on cybersecurity and artificial intelligence strategies welcomes academics, researchers, students, and professionals from around the world. Our goal is to support broad scientific participation through flexible registration options and clear, affordable fees. The following table summarizes registration and publication fees. All payments must be completed in full to confirm your registration and participation.
Non-speaking participants: Free
Category | Fees/Accepted paper | Notes
Non-speaking Participants (no paper) | Free | Participation without paper submission
Paper Publication Fee | $150 | Mandatory for all accepted papers
Graduate Students | $100 | Discounted publication fee for current students
Al-Mansour University College Researchers | $75 | 50% exemption on standard publication fees
International Researchers | Free |
To ensure high quality and consistency in the final proceedings, all accepted papers will be forwarded to an English Proofreading Office prior to publication. This proofreading service is a mandatory process and requires an additional fee (applied separately from the publication fee). Authors will receive detailed instructions about English proofreading charges and payment after acceptance of their paper. More related information are as follows:
At least one author of each accepted paper must pay the publication fee for the paper to be included in the conference program and proceedings.
At least one author of each accepted paper must register and present the paper at the conference.
Payment of the required fee is necessary for final confirmation of your registration and inclusion in the proceedings.
Fees are non-refundable once paid.

Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Call for papers
Call for Paper
The organizing committee of the CAIS conference, organized by
Al-Mansour University College
in Baghdad, is pleased to invite academics, researchers and practitioners from all over the world to participate by submitting their scientific research. This conference will be held on
September 23-24, 2026
at the
Al Mansour Melia Hotel
, in the center of the Iraqi capital, Baghdad, on the banks of the Tigris River, where the event brings together thought leaders and experts in the fields of artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. It provides a distinguished academic and professional platform that brings together researchers, academics, industry experts, and practitioners from around the world to exchange ideas, present original research, and discuss emerging trends in artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. The conference emphasizes both theoretical foundations and practical applications, with a strong focus on cutting-edge research, real-world deployment, and policy implications, aiming to foster innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and the development of robust, intelligent, and secure digital systems.
Participants will have the opportunity to engage in high-level technical discussions, build professional networks, and explore future research directions. The conference welcomes high-quality, original contributions that have not been previously published and are not under consideration elsewhere. All accepted papers will be published in the official conference proceedings and submitted for indexing in reputable academic databases, subject to publisher policies.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies

. Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Program
However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(

is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies

. Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.

14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(
lead organizer): MUC
is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
(CAIS-26)
. MUC is a respected higher education institution committed to advancing scientific research and fostering cooperation between academia and industry in fields including cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. This role reflects the college’s mission to support innovation, knowledge exchange, and capacity building within Iraq and the broader scientific community.
Systems (Zesys)
,
(a partner):
the Center for the Advancement of Embedded Systems (often associated with Zesys) is an organization focused on embedded systems research, education, and technology innovation. It works to advance hardware-software integration, support smart system development, and foster industry-academia collaboration. Its partnership with CAIS-26 strengthens the conference’s technical scope, particularly in areas where cybersecurity intersects with intelligent and embedded computing technologies.

Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Program
However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(

is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
(CAIS-26)
. This role reflects the college’s mission to support innovation, knowledge exchange, and capacity building within Iraq and the broader scientific community.

Its partnership with CAIS-26 strengthens the conference’s technical scope, particularly in areas where cybersecurity intersects with intelligent and embedded computing technologies.

Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Program
Program
However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

Airport pick‑up and drop‑off.
Ground transportation for all listed places in the conference program.
Daily meals for international authors during stay (breakfast, lunch).
On‑site coordination and local support.
Not Included

Private taxis or activities outside the conference program.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.

Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(

is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
(CAIS-26)
. Guide for Authors
Information
for Authors: Submitting scientific papers according to Springer instructions

​
The Organizing Committee of the
CAIS’26
is pleased to invite researchers, academics, and practitioners to submit their original scientific contributions. The conference proceedings will follow the publication standards of
Springer
, one of the world’s leading academic publishers. To ensure a high-quality scientific outcome and smooth publication process, all submissions must strictly comply with the technical, ethical, and formatting requirements defined below. Authors are strongly encouraged to carefully read these instructions before preparing and submitting their manuscripts.   All submitted papers must be prepared using the
official Springer paper template
corresponding to the
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research(ACSAR)
series. This ensures consistency in formatting and compatibility with the final publication process. Authors are invited to adhering to Springer template which can be downloaded
here
.
Manuscript Preparation Guidelines
Papers must be written in
clear, precise, and professional English
, using correct grammar and academic writing conventions.
Submissions should demonstrate a
sound scientific structure
, including a clear problem statement, methodology, results, and conclusions.
The acceptable length of submissions is:
Short papers:
minimum of
6+ pages
Regular papers:
minimum of
12+ pages
Figures, tables, and equations must be clearly labeled, properly referenced in the text, and of sufficient quality for publication.
All references must follow the
Springer citation style
and include complete bibliographic information.
Ethical and Originality Requirements
Submitted papers must represent
original work
that has not been previously published or submitted elsewhere.
Any form of plagiarism, including self-plagiarism, is strictly prohibited.
All authors listed on the paper must have made a
significant scientific contribution
to the work.
Instructions for Evaluating and Selecting Papers
All submitted manuscripts undergo a
rigorous, transparent, and unbiased evaluation process
supervised by the Conference Program Committee Chairs. The evaluation process is designed to ensure that accepted papers meet international scientific and publication standards. The selection process is carried out in multiple phases. During the initial evaluation phase, each submitted manuscript is screened to verify compliance with the
Springer ACSAR formatting requirements
, r
elevance to the
core themes of the conference
(i.e., Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, and related interdisciplinary applications), and the plagiarism rate below 15%. Submissions that fail to meet these initial requirements will be
excluded at this stage
and will not proceed to peer review.
Papers that pass the initial screening are forwarded to the
conference reviewers
, composed of recognized experts in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and related fields. Each paper is reviewed based on the following criteria.
Firstly, originality
(i.e.,
the paper must present novel ideas, methods, or applications that contribute meaningfully to the field and have not been published previously).
Secondly, scientific quality
where the research must demonstrate methodological rigor, logical consistency, appropriate data analysis, and well-supported conclusions.
Thirdly, significance
of
t
he contribution that should have clear academic or practical value, with potential impact on future research, technological development, or real-world applications.
Lastly
,
clarity
of
manuscript
presentation
that should be well-structured, clearly written, and easy to follow. Figures, tables, and explanations should effectively support the scientific content. Based on the reviewers’ recommendations, papers with
high scientific merit and strong relevance
to the conference themes will be selected for acceptance and publication in the conference proceedings.
Publication and Communication
Accepted papers will be included in the official conference proceedings, subject to successful completion of all required revisions and administrative procedures. Authors will receive detailed feedback and revision instructions, if applicable. For inquiries related to
paper formatting
,
submission procedures
,
or evaluation guidelines
, authors are encouraged to contact the conference committee through the official conference email address.

Call for papers
Call for Paper
The organizing committee of the CAIS conference, organized by
Al-Mansour University College
in Baghdad, is pleased to invite academics, researchers and practitioners from all over the world to participate by submitting their scientific research. This conference will be held on
September 23-24, 2026
at the
Al Mansour Melia Hotel
, in the center of the Iraqi capital, Baghdad, on the banks of the Tigris River, where the event brings together thought leaders and experts in the fields of artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. It provides a distinguished academic and professional platform that brings together researchers, academics, industry experts, and practitioners from around the world to exchange ideas, present original research, and discuss emerging trends in artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. The conference emphasizes both theoretical foundations and practical applications, with a strong focus on cutting-edge research, real-world deployment, and policy implications, aiming to foster innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and the development of robust, intelligent, and secure digital systems.
Participants will have the opportunity to engage in high-level technical discussions, build professional networks, and explore future research directions. The conference welcomes high-quality, original contributions that have not been previously published and are not under consideration elsewhere. All accepted papers will be published in the official conference proceedings and submitted for indexing in reputable academic databases, subject to publisher policies.
Technical Tracks and Topics of Interest
CAIS 2026 is organized around two primary technical tracks. Submissions are welcomed in, but not limited to, the following areas:
Track 1:
Cryptography
Key management
Public key (asymmetric) techniques
Symmetric cryptography and hash functions
Cryptanalysis and other attacks
Information-theoretic techniques
Mathematical foundations of cryptography
Formal methods and theory of security

Program
However, the conference will provide a social atmosphere for all participants during and after the conference by arranging a sightseeing tour to the most historical cities in Iraq (Mesopotamia). Where, all the participants will be able to visit the ancient Babylon city and see its archaeological landmarks. Babylon city was the capital of the Babylonians during the reign of Hammurabi, where the Babylonians ruled the Mesopotamia. The detailed tour program for 3-days afternoon (23–25 September 2026) (Baghdad / Babylon) is as follows:
Date (2026) | Day | Time (Baghdad, UTC+3) | Activity | Meeting point / Notes
23 Sep 2026 | Day 1 (Wed) | 14:00–14:15 | Meet & welcome — short briefing | Mustansiriya Madrasa entrance (left bank of the Tigris).
14:15–15:30 | Guided visit — Mustansiriya Madrasa (historic madrasah, architecture, photo stops) | Please wear comfortable shoes; interior guided tour.
15:30–16:00 | Walk / transfer to Al-Mutanabbi Street | Short transfer along the riverfront.
16:00–18:30 | Stroll Al-Mutanabbi Street — bookshops, cafés, street culture; photo stops | Time for buying books, visiting a café; relaxed walk.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — nearby cultural restaurant/café | End of Day 1 — group dinner included.
24 Sep 2026 | Day 2 Thu | 13:30–14:30 | Depart hotel / transfer to Kadhimiya | Allow traffic time; conservative dress recommended for shrines.
14:30–16:00 | Visit Al-Kadhimayn (shrine of Imams, incl. Imam Muhammad al-Jawad) — guided respectful visit | 
16:30–18:30 | Abu Nuwas / riverside stroll (Abu Nuwas Park & Tigris promenade) — photos & leisure | Riverside walk; good spot for evening photos.
18:30–20:00 | Free meal provided (dinner) — riverside restaurant | End of Day 2 — group dinner included.
25 Sep 2026 | Day 3 Fri | 08:00 | Conference programme begins — depart for Babylon (conference group) | Drive from Baghdad to Babylon (~2 hours by road).
10:30–13:00 | Guided visit — Babylon archaeological site (Ishtar Gate area, ruins, highlights) | Local guide at site; walk on uneven surfaces.
13:00–14:00 | Photo session & wrap-up (group photos, graduation / conference photos) | Time reserved for extended photography and group shots.
14:00–15:00 | Free meal provided (lunch) — at site or nearby Hillah restaurant | End of Day 3 programme (and end of conference field visit).
Quick logistics & practical notes
Transport:
Group minibus/coach recommended for Day 2 and the Baghdad→Babylon transfer (Day 3). Allow ~2 hours Baghdad→Babylon by road.
Dress & conduct:
Modest clothing at religious sites; women should have headscarf available for shrine areas. Follow shrine staff instructions.
Accessibility:
Some historic sites have uneven ground; comfortable shoes and a hat/sunscreen recommended.
Meals:
Each day ends with a free group meal (organizer / conference provides). If you want vegetarian / dietary options.

About the Conference
The 3
rd
International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies (CAIS)
is a global event specialized in discussing the latest developments and strategies related to two of the most advanced and interesting fields today: Artificial Intelligence and Cybersecurity. The conference seeks to enhance the in-depth understanding of contemporary challenges in the field of digital security and provide innovative solutions through artificial intelligence technologies, allowing academics, researchers and practitioners the opportunity to communicate, exchange ideas and collaborate to enhance the security of digital systems and innovate smart applications that contribute to protecting privacy and achieving data integrity.
It is planned to publish the proceedings with Springer in their
Advances in Computer Science Applications and Research (ACSAR).
Vision
The vision of the conference is to establish a deep knowledge base that combines artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, with the aim of enhancing digital security and providing innovative technical solutions, which contributes to achieving a safe and sustainable technical environment that relies on artificial intelligence as an effective means to address modern electronic challenges.
Objectives
The conference aims to enhance academic and industrial cooperation in the fields related to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, as it seeks to present the latest research and practical applications concerned with digital security and privacy protection. The conference also aims to support the exchange of ideas and experiences in areas such as encryption, key management, and network security, and to provide in-depth studies on the use of artificial intelligence in the fields of natural language processing, computer vision, and machine learning methods to enhance security.
Organizing body
Al-Mansour University College (MUC)
(
lead organizer): MUC
is the principal organizer of the 3rd International Conference on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Strategies
(CAIS-26)
. MUC is a respected higher education institution committed to advancing scientific research and fostering cooperation between academia and industry in fields including cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies. This role reflects the college’s mission to support innovation, knowledge exchange, and capacity building within Iraq and the broader scientific community.
Systems (Zesys)
,
(a partner):
the Center for the Advancement of Embedded Systems (often associated with Zesys) is an organization focused on embedded systems research, education, and technology innovation. It works to advance hardware-software integration, support smart system development, and foster industry-academia collaboration. Its partnership with CAIS-26 strengthens the conference’s technical scope, particularly in areas where cybersecurity intersects with intelligent and embedded computing technologies.

:
the Iraqi Association for Libraries and Information is a professional society dedicated to supporting library and information science in Iraq. The group brings together specialists in information management, documentation, and librarianship to promote best practices, professional development, and collaboration across educational and research institutions. Their involvement in CAIS-26 underscores the importance of information access and management in academic and technical research contexts.
